skill.name: plan-writing
skill.description: Create concise implementation plans with specific tasks, dependencies, and verification checks for features, fixes, refactors, and new projects.
skill.summary: Create short, verifiable plans for multi-step coding and project work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-cryptography terms, but review found no executable code, cryptographic implementation, network access, or credential handling. The command-related hits are documentation examples for planning and verification, so publication is acceptable with a low-risk note.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected inline code and fenced Markdown as Ruby or shell execution. Review shows these locations contain filenames, example commands, and plan templates, not executable skill code. This is a false positive, but generated plans may mention commands that users should review before running.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown guidance and examples. No script file, tool invocation, or executable code path is present in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at descriptive prose lines. Review found no hashing, encryption, signature validation, or crypto library usage at these locations. This is a false positive with no confirmed security impact.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language description and planning guidance. There is no cryptographic operation or dependency to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Complex coding work can fail when tasks, dependencies, and checks are vague. This skill turns a goal into a short, ordered plan with clear verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a coding goal into a concise sequence of tasks.
Adds verification criteria to each planned task.
Adapts plan structure for new projects, features, bug fixes, and refactors.
Highlights task dependencies and keeps final verification last.
Encourages project-specific commands instead of generic script lists.
limitations: It does not execute the plan or validate commands automatically.
It depends on accurate project context from the user or workspace.
It is optimized for short plans, not full project roadmaps.
It does not replace security, accessibility, or production readiness review.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Turn a feature request into ordered tasks with file targets, dependencies, and checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactor
use_cases.description: Define focused refactor steps and verification checks before changing multiple files.

use_cases.title: Scope a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Capture the root cause, affected files, fix steps, and test criteria in one short plan.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short implementation plan for this task: [describe task]. Include the goal, 5 to 8 tasks, and a done checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a concise plan for [feature or fix]. For each task, include a specific verification step and keep final verification last.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor plan for [area]. Identify affected files, dependencies, task order, risk points, and focused verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan From Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning with existing project details
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the relevant project context for [goal], then write a one-page plan with task order, project-specific commands, and done criteria.

output_examples.input: Add password reset to an existing web app.
output: Goal: Add a password reset flow for existing users.
Tasks: inspect current auth flow, add reset token storage, add request endpoint, add confirmation endpoint, add user interface, verify email and token behavior.
Done when: reset email sends, valid tokens work once, expired tokens fail, and tests cover the main path.
Goal: Simplify the shared API client without changing behavior.
Tasks: identify callers, extract request helpers, update imports, preserve error handling, run targeted tests.
Done when: all callers use the new helper and existing API tests pass.

output_examples.input: Fix a dark mode toggle bug.
output: Goal: Make the dark mode toggle persist across reloads.
Tasks: reproduce the bug, inspect storage logic, update state initialization, test reload behavior, check visual state.
Done when: the selected theme remains active after refresh and the toggle matches the current theme.

best_practices: Start with one clear goal before listing tasks.
Keep plans short enough to review in one page.
Use verification checks that match the project and the change.
anti_patterns: Do not create long plans with many nested subtasks.
Do not add generic scripts that do not apply to the project.
Do not treat the plan as complete without clear done criteria.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create short implementation plans with tasks, dependencies, and verification checks.

faq.question: Can it write full project documentation?
faq.answer: It can support documentation, but it is focused on concise task plans.

faq.question: Does it run commands or modify files by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning guidance and may suggest commands for the user to review.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before implementing a feature, fixing a complex bug, or refactoring several files.

faq.question: How detailed should the plan be?
faq.answer: The plan should usually fit on one page with 5 to 10 clear tasks.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
